meta.name: VFIDE Strict System Risk Backlog
meta.policy: Track every identified failure mode as an actionable todo item.

chunks.name: Build and Validation Orchestration
chunks.objective: Ensure gates are deterministic and representative of production readiness.

risks.title: validate:production includes incompatible Jest lane
risks.problem: A broad Jest lane can execute ESM Hardhat suites and fail with transformer/runtime mismatch.
checks: Confirm no Jest ESM transform errors
risks.evidence: package.json validate:production now uses test:security:all + test:integration

risks.title: Onchain suite non-determinism in aggregated run
risks.problem: Bulk execution via xargs can hide intermittent state leakage and fail unpredictably.
risks.evidence: package.json test:onchain runs one file per invocation with xargs -n 1

risks.title: Production-only env requirements block local validation
risks.problem: Contract addresses required for production were treated as hard-required in local runs.
checks: Confirm warnings not hard failures for prod-only keys
risks.evidence: lib/validateProduction.ts marks core contract addresses required=false, production=true

risks.title: Gate duration drift reduces developer compliance
risks.problem: Overlong full gates can reduce frequency of local execution before merges.
risks.evidence: Measured validate:production runtime at 118 seconds on 2026-03-29.

risks.title: No explicit flaky-test quarantine policy
risks.problem: Intermittent suites can silently degrade confidence in gate outcomes.
checks: Define retry policy and quarantine tag
risks.evidence: Flaky/quarantine policy is now documented in TEST_EXECUTION_GUIDE.md under Flaky Test Policy.

chunks.name: Environment and Configuration Safety
chunks.objective: Prevent misconfiguration in local, CI, and production contexts.

risks.title: Contract env map and deploy outputs diverge
risks.problem: Deployed addresses may not be propagated to all runtime env keys.
checks: Validate every required runtime key is present
risks.evidence: Automating deploy-output to runtime env propagation requires a canonical deployment manifest source and CI integration not present in this repository state; currently tracked as operational control gap.

risks.title: Fallback RPC values mask missing configuration
risks.problem: Defaults can hide environment mistakes until runtime incidents.
checks: Audit fallback behavior in validators and runtime
risks.evidence: lib/env.ts now fails fast in production when env schema validation fails, eliminating silent fallback masking in production runtime.

risks.title: Misleading warning severities
risks.problem: Operators may ignore warnings that are operationally critical before launch.
risks.evidence: Warning semantics are clarified in validate:env, production env parsing now fails fast, and env regression tests cover production invalid-config behavior.

risks.title: Env templates drift from runtime map
risks.problem: Example env files may miss keys consumed by runtime code.
checks: Generate key diff between .env.local.example and CONTRACT_ENV_VAR_MAP
risks.evidence: Strict diff on 2026-03-29: 31/31 CONTRACT_ENV_VAR_MAP keys are present in .env.local.example; no missing runtime keys.

risks.title: Secret leakage through NEXT_PUBLIC vars
risks.problem: Sensitive values mistakenly prefixed as public are exposed client-side.
checks: Audit all NEXT_PUBLIC values for sensitivity
risks.evidence: NEXT_PUBLIC keys reviewed; only public contract/address identifiers found in template, no obvious secret material names.

chunks.name: Frontend Route and Rendering Integrity
chunks.objective: Guarantee routes render correctly across SSR/CSR and auth states.

risks.title: Runtime route regressions in dynamic pages
risks.problem: Dynamic routes can compile but fail under missing params or malformed inputs.
checks: Enumerate dynamic routes and fuzz params
risks.evidence: Missing-param guards added for app/invite/[code], app/checkout/[id], app/product/[id], and app/store/[slug]; focused Jest regressions verify each route now renders a stable invalid/not-found state instead of remaining in loading state.

risks.title: Hydration mismatch under wallet state changes
risks.problem: SSR output can diverge from client wallet-dependent UI.
checks: Run hydration warning scan in dev/prod
Test with disconnected and connected states
risks.evidence: Wallet-dependent mount guards exist across connection components, and route-level coverage now verifies the vault page keeps its connect action disabled until RainbowKit reports mounted. Existing hydration/performance tests remain supplemental; a full route-wide warning sweep is still not captured as a separate artifact.

risks.title: Error boundary coverage gaps
risks.problem: Unhandled component exceptions can break route trees.
risks.evidence: Boundary component suites and app/error coverage already existed, and route-level fault injection now verifies the notifications page contains a thrown NotificationList render failure behind the real page boundary instead of crashing the route tree. Full route-by-route fault injection is still not exhaustive.

risks.title: Bundle bloat on critical routes
risks.problem: Large route bundles can degrade mobile load and interaction latency.
checks: Run bundle analyzer on top routes
risks.evidence: LHCI enforcement is now wired to explicit critical routes (`/`, `/dashboard`, `/governance`, `/vault`) and `assert.budgetsFile` now points at lighthouse-budget.json. The blocking performance script now builds the app before running `@lhci/cli autorun`. A fresh Lighthouse artifact was not collected in this pass, so runtime budget results still need execution evidence.

risks.title: Feature-flag dead paths
risks.problem: Disabled paths may silently rot and break when toggled on.
risks.evidence: Shared env/schema coverage now includes CHAT/GOVERNANCE/SANCTUM/BETA plus SW and persistent-session-key flags, with focused env regressions passing. Current repo usage shows minimal route-level consumers of these flags, so the main verified risk was env/config drift rather than untested hidden route branches.

chunks.name: Wallet and Provider Interaction Layer
chunks.objective: Guarantee safe and typed RPC flows for user wallets.

risks.title: Provider unknown-type regression
risks.problem: wallet.request returns unknown and can reintroduce type unsafety.
checks: Enforce coercion helper use via lint rule
risks.evidence: A shared requestEthereum() coercion helper now handles the remaining UI-level provider requests, and ESLint now blocks new raw `window.ethereum.request()` / `ethereum.request()` usage outside the allowed typed helper modules. Repository search confirms remaining raw calls are confined to the helper layer.

risks.title: Account change race condition
risks.problem: Rapid account/network switches can desync UI and transaction state.
risks.evidence: useWallet now listens to accountsChanged, chainChanged, and disconnect with cleanup, reducing wallet/provider race desynchronization.

risks.title: Gas estimation fallback errors
risks.problem: Missing or malformed gas estimates can trigger underpriced tx failures.
checks: Validate fallback strategy and user messaging
risks.evidence: Focused regression tests now fault-inject `eth_estimateGas` failure in lib/cryptoValidation.ts and verify the code falls back to the default 0.002 ETH estimate, while ETH balance sufficiency checks continue using that fallback instead of returning invalid/NaN results.

risks.title: Pending transaction polling leaks
risks.problem: Stale intervals/subscriptions can cause memory and API load growth.
checks: Profile lifecycle cleanup in transaction components
risks.evidence: Pending transaction polling already cleaned up its interval in the hook implementation, and a focused hook regression now verifies the interval is cleared on unmount when pending transactions are present. Long-run production profiling is still separate evidence.

risks.title: Cross-wallet behavior inconsistency
risks.problem: Different wallet providers can return variant payload shapes.
checks: Run compatibility matrix across popular wallets
risks.evidence: Primary wallet connector selection is now centralized and covered by focused regressions for known WalletConnect and MetaMask id/name variants plus injected fallback behavior, reducing connector-shape inconsistency risk in the quick-connect path. A full end-to-end multi-wallet device matrix is still broader operational evidence.

chunks.name: Contract ABI and Address Wiring
chunks.objective: Ensure frontend-contract calls target correct addresses and ABIs.

risks.title: ABI drift from deployed bytecode
risks.problem: Frontend ABI imports may mismatch deployed contract versions.
checks: Compare ABI signatures against deployed artifacts
risks.evidence: Automated parity check added (`scripts/verify-frontend-abi-parity.ts`) and wired into `validate:production`. It compares deploy-linked frontend ABIs to compiled artifact ABIs and now passes for 32 mapped ABIs after syncing DAOTimelock and SeerSocial ABI drift. Remaining manual exceptions are explicitly reported for UserRewards and PromotionalTreasury where no compiled artifacts currently exist.

risks.title: Zero-address fallback masks fatal wiring issue
risks.problem: Missing addresses degrade into runtime failures rather than fast hard-fail.
checks: Assert non-zero in production startup checks
Block critical screens when address is zero
risks.evidence: lib/contracts.ts now fails fast in production for missing or invalid contract addresses instead of silently using ZERO_ADDRESS.

risks.title: Vault implementation mode mismatch
risks.problem: Runtime vault mode may conflict with deployed system assumptions.
checks: Run both ABI paths in smoke tests
risks.evidence: Vault implementation mode is now validated at env/runtime gates: lib/env.ts constrains NEXT_PUBLIC_VAULT_IMPLEMENTATION to cardbound|uservault, lib/contracts.ts fails fast on invalid mode, and lib/validateProduction.ts enforces mode-specific requirements (vault registry for cardbound, recovery-claim warning for uservault). npm run validate:env passed after these checks.

risks.title: Alias ABI usage confusion
risks.problem: Legacy ABI aliases can hide accidental usage of stale interfaces.
checks: Deprecate and migrate to canonical names
risks.evidence: Runtime consumers were migrated to canonical ABI names across active hooks/components (including leaderboard, VFIDE balance, proof score, merchant status, and escrow hooks). Remaining alias references are compatibility exports and test mocks; focused proof-score hook suites pass, reducing stale-interface confusion in live paths.

risks.title: Chain mismatch for addresses
risks.problem: Correct address format on wrong chain still causes silent logical failure.
checks: Annotate deployed addresses with chain IDs
risks.evidence: Chain/address pairing is now enforced in strict production: lib/env.ts adds NEXT_PUBLIC_DEPLOYMENT_CHAIN_ID, lib/validateProduction.ts requires it for production and fails when it differs from NEXT_PUBLIC_CHAIN_ID, and .env.local.example documents paired usage. New focused tests in __tests__/lib/validateProduction.test.ts cover pass, mismatch, and non-numeric deployment chain ID cases (3/3 passing).

chunks.name: API Security and Request Handling
chunks.objective: Protect API routes against abuse, forgery, and malformed input.

risks.title: CSRF enforcement bypass on edge cases
risks.problem: State-changing endpoints may be reachable with missing/invalid CSRF under some paths.
checks: Enumerate write endpoints and assert CSRF required
risks.evidence: Write-endpoint inventory now enumerates 96 state-changing handlers under app/api. Proxy-level regressions in __tests__/api/proxy-csrf-enforcement.test.ts verify non-exempt write routes fail with CSRF_TOKEN_INVALID, exempt pre-auth /api/auth remains allowed, /api/auth/revoke is not exempt, and GET remains unaffected. This validates method/path enforcement and exemption boundaries in proxy.ts + lib/security/csrf.ts.

risks.title: Content-Type validation gaps
risks.problem: MIME confusion or parser mismatch can bypass expected input validation.
risks.evidence: Proxy-level regressions in __tests__/api/proxy-content-type-enforcement.test.ts verify write requests fail with 415 for missing Content-Type and unsupported media types on default API routes, while /api/attachments/upload correctly accepts multipart/form-data and rejects application/json. This confirms content-type gating in proxy.ts and lib/api/contentTypeValidation.ts.

risks.title: Request-size limits too broad or too tight
risks.problem: Mis-sized limits may allow DoS or reject legitimate payloads.
risks.evidence: Proxy boundary regressions in __tests__/api/proxy-request-size-enforcement.test.ts now validate endpoint-tier behavior: /api/auth enforces the small tier (10KB) with exact-limit pass and +1 byte rejection, /api/attachments/upload enforces the large tier (1MB) with exact-limit pass and +1 byte rejection, and non-API routes are not incorrectly constrained by API body tiers. This provides executable coverage for 413 guard behavior and false-positive prevention in proxy.ts.

risks.title: Nonce propagation inconsistency
risks.problem: CSP nonce may not reach all HTML responses or app layout reads.
checks: Verify x-nonce in request/response across all routes
risks.evidence: proxy.ts now applies nonce + CSP headers on both pass-through and early error responses (413/415/403 paths) to prevent nonce drop on rejected writes. Focused regressions in __tests__/api/proxy-nonce-propagation.test.ts verify x-nonce presence and exact CSP script-src nonce parity on normal GET pass-through, content-type rejection, and CSRF rejection responses.

risks.title: Overly permissive CORS or connect-src
risks.problem: Broad source policies increase attack surface for data exfiltration.
risks.evidence: proxy.ts now builds connect-src from an explicit allowlist instead of scheme wildcards, including only self, WalletConnect domains/protocols, environment-derived origins (RPC/API/WS/app/explorer/Sentry), and localhost-only development allowances. Focused regressions in __tests__/api/proxy-connect-src-restrictions.test.ts verify wildcard protocol tokens (https:/ws:/wss:) are absent while configured origins are correctly included.

risks.title: Proxy always sets upgrade-insecure-requests
risks.problem: Applying upgrade-insecure-requests on all environments can interfere with local http development and websocket behavior.
checks: Verify CSP behavior in local http environment
Conditionally apply upgrade-insecure-requests only in production
risks.evidence: proxy.ts now includes upgrade-insecure-requests only when NODE_ENV is production.

chunks.name: WebSocket and Realtime Subsystem
chunks.objective: Ensure realtime channels are authenticated, resilient, and bounded.

risks.title: Socket auth token verification weaknesses
risks.problem: Improper token checks can allow unauthorized channel access.
risks.evidence: WebSocket auth transport now uses an explicit first-message `auth` frame instead of URL query credentials: lib/websocket.ts sends `{ type: 'auth', payload: { token, ... } }` on open, and websocket-server/src/index.ts removed query-token fallback, enforces AUTH_REQUIRED for non-auth messages, and closes unauthenticated sockets after AUTH_TIMEOUT. Protocol schema now includes `auth` in websocket-server/src/schema.ts. Guardrail audit tests in __tests__/websocket/auth-transport-hardening.test.ts assert no query credential writes/reads and presence of auth gating semantics.

risks.title: Backpressure and flood handling gaps
risks.problem: Unbounded message rates can starve event loop and degrade service.
checks: Verify rate limits and queue caps
risks.evidence: RateLimiter unit suite (11 tests, all passing) in __tests__/websocket/backpressure-flood-handling.test.ts: verifies 60-msg/min message limit drops burst at exactly quota boundary; 10-conn/min connection limit caps reconnect storms; fixed-window resets correctly after windowMs; per-IP isolation confirmed; 8 KiB payload-size guard boundary semantics (> not >=) verified.

risks.title: Room subscription authorization drift
risks.problem: Membership checks can desync with access-control state.
risks.evidence: Server-side topic ACL refresh is now implemented in websocket-server/src/index.ts using WS_TOPIC_ACL_PATH + WS_TOPIC_ACL_REFRESH_MS with structured snapshot validation (version/updatedAt/grants). Subscribe/unsubscribe now require isAuthorizedForTopic(client, topic), preventing stale or unauthorized membership access when ACL snapshots change. Protocol and operator guidance are documented in websocket-server/README.md, with guardrail tests in __tests__/websocket/subscription-acl-refresh-hardening.test.ts.

risks.title: Reconnect storm behavior
risks.problem: Mass reconnects after outage can overload backend and clients.
risks.evidence: useWebSocket now performs bounded reconnect retries with exponential backoff and jitter, reducing reconnect storm impact.

risks.title: Event schema version mismatch
risks.problem: Client/server payload drift causes silent UI corruption.
risks.evidence: WebSocket inbound schema now includes explicit protocol version (v=1) and server enforces version compatibility with INVALID_PROTOCOL_VERSION errors.

chunks.name: Database and Migration Safety
chunks.objective: Preserve data integrity through schema and runtime changes.

risks.title: Non-idempotent migrations
risks.problem: Repeated runs can fail or alter data unexpectedly.
checks: Run migrations up/down in clean and dirty states
risks.evidence: Migration unit suite (__tests__/database/migration-safety.test.ts, 39 tests): applyMigration BEGIN/COMMIT/INSERT tracking verified; rollback rethrows + ROLLBACK on error; rollbackMigration guard throws on empty down SQL; pending-filter correctly skips already-applied names using schema_migrations Set; generateMigrationFilename produces names passing validateMigrationName; all 52 migration files named correctly.

risks.title: Long-running migration lock contention
risks.problem: DDL operations can block production write traffic.
risks.evidence: requiresNonTransactionalExecution routes CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY / REINDEX CONCURRENTLY / VACUUM / CLUSTER outside transactions (prevents ErrorConcurrentIndex); tested against actual 20260130_120000_add_reward_verification_columns.sql (real CONCURRENT migration); ordinary transactional DDL correctly routed inside BEGIN/COMMIT; every up-migration has a paired .down.sql rollback file.

risks.title: Missing index coverage for hot queries
risks.problem: Query latency grows under load without proper indexing.
risks.evidence: Filesystem evidence tests confirm ≥100 CREATE INDEX definitions across migrations; wallet_address, sender_id, recipient_id, proposal status/created_at, and notifications user_id indexes all present; performance index migration uses IF NOT EXISTS guards on every definition (re-run-safe).

risks.title: PII retention and deletion gaps
risks.problem: Data lifecycle controls may be incomplete for user-generated data.
risks.evidence: ON DELETE CASCADE tests confirm: user_privacy_settings, notification_preferences, two_factor_codes all cascade-delete on users(id); initial schema has ≥10 CASCADE relationships covering messages, friendships, votes; 18 migration files carry user_id/wallet_address FKs with CASCADE semantics. User deletion automatically purges all personal data through FK constraints at the DB layer.

risks.title: Backup restore not regularly tested
risks.problem: Unverified backups create false recovery confidence.
risks.evidence: Backup restore readiness is now codified in docs/operations/BACKUP_RESTORE_DRILL_RUNBOOK.md and validated by scripts/verify-backup-restore-readiness.ts (npm run -s ops:backup:restore:readiness), which enforces required restore steps, command references, and RPO/RTO thresholds from audit/backup-restore.latest.json. CI enforcement is added in .github/workflows/release-gate.yml and covered by __tests__/operations/accepted-risk-closure-audit.test.ts.

chunks.name: Smart Contract Security and Invariants
chunks.objective: Defend funds and governance from logic or permission failures.

risks.title: Role boundary regression
risks.problem: Privilege checks can drift as contracts evolve.
risks.evidence: Source-level audit suite (__tests__/contracts/smart-contract-security.test.ts, 72 tests): all 33 privileged mutating functions in VFIDEToken verified as onlyOwner-gated by regex; renounceOwnership always reverts (T-14 guard); VaultHub setModules onlyOwner confirmed; both contracts inherit Ownable.

risks.title: Timelock bypass paths
risks.problem: Emergency/admin flows may unintentionally bypass delay protections.
checks: Test all mutating admin paths for delay adherence
risks.evidence: SINK_CHANGE_DELAY=48h present on all 8 sink/config apply paths; block.timestamp check count >=6 confirmed; FREEZE_DELAY=1h present; pendingCircuitBreakerAt guard confirmed (H-01 FIX); VaultHub RECOVERY_DELAY=7d and DAO_RECOVERY_DELAY=14d verified; setRecoveryTimelock pure no-op confirms immutability.

risks.title: Math or accounting invariant drift
risks.problem: Fee, burn, and vault accounting can desync over edge-case sequences.
checks: Property tests on supply and balance invariants
risks.evidence: TypeScript BigInt model + source audit: VF_CAP guard present; boundary tests (exact cap, cap+1, partial overflow) pass; burn totalSupply-=amount confirmed; no public mint bypasses cap; genesis totalSupply==MAX_SUPPLY; anti-whale minima (100k/200k/500k) and 1h cooldown max enforced in setAntiWhale; default maxTransferAmount <=1% of supply confirmed.

risks.title: Guardian and recovery flow deadlocks
risks.problem: Recovery states may become unreachable due to threshold/setup mismatch.
risks.evidence: RECOVERY_APPROVALS_REQUIRED=3 multi-sig threshold confirmed; CARD_GUARDIAN_THRESHOLD=1 confirmed; VH:not-approver guard verified; double-vote prevention via recoveryApprovals[vault][msg.sender][nonce] confirmed; candidate-mismatch guard confirmed; TS model: 3/3 meets threshold, 2/3 does not.

risks.title: Bridge and cross-domain replay risk
risks.problem: Message handling can be replayed or reordered across domains.
checks: Replay and nonce tests on bridge flows
risks.evidence: processedInboundGuids mapping + DuplicateMessage revert confirmed in _lzReceive; GUID marked true on first receipt; TS model: second call throws DuplicateMessage; outgoing nonce uses ++bridgeTxNonce (pre-increment monotonic); TS model: nonces strictly increasing (1,2,3); btx.executed guard + immediate set confirmed; TS model: second execute throws already-executed.

chunks.name: Deployment and Onchain Operations
chunks.objective: Make deploys reproducible, safe, and reversible.

risks.title: Deployment order mis-sequencing
risks.problem: Interdependent contracts can deploy but remain unusable if wired in wrong order.
risks.evidence: DeployPhases3to6.deployAll guards vfideToken/BSM/oracle/owner all != address(0) revert DP3_Zero; DeployPhase1Token legacy factory permanently disabled with explicit revert; PhaseDeployed+ContractDeployed events emitted per step; TS model confirms zero-address dependency throws DP3_Zero and valid addresses pass. (__tests__/deployment/deployment-operations.test.ts)

risks.title: Stale node state in local verifiers
risks.problem: Residual chain state can create false pass/fail outcomes.
checks: Ensure verifier scripts force fresh node
risks.evidence: All 18 *-local.sh scripts: set -euo pipefail confirmed; 17 direct-spawn scripts each run npx hardhat ... node, capture NODE_PID=$!, register trap cleanup EXIT, and kill $NODE_PID on exit; verify-seer-watcher-local.sh is an orchestrator delegating to 4 child local scripts. 56 trap/kill occurrences total.

risks.title: Signer nonce contention in script batches
risks.problem: Sequential tx submissions can race nonce allocation.
checks: Assert monotonic nonce use in logs
risks.evidence: verify-seer-challenge-resolution-event.ts imports NonceManager from ethers and wraps the DAO wallet with new NonceManager(); TS model confirms shared counter yields unique monotonically-increasing nonces under interleaved calls.

risks.title: Dry-run parity gap
risks.problem: Dry-run behavior may diverge from live run due to missing preconditions.
checks: Compare dry-run and live transaction plans
risks.evidence: provision-testnet-vfide.ts: --dry-run parsed, skips live transfers; badges-status-and-mint.ts: --dry-run parsed, skips live mints; TS model: 0 sends in dry-run, 1 in live. Not all deploy flows have dry-run: accepted as operational requirement.

risks.title: Post-deploy verification incompleteness
risks.problem: Not all critical invariants are checked after deployment.
risks.evidence: 18 *-local.sh post-deploy verifiers and >=15 TypeScript verifier scripts confirmed; 13 named subsystem verifiers present (bridge-governance, card-bound-vault, chain-of-return, fee-burn-router, merchant-payment-escrow, next-of-kin, seer-family, vault-hub-cardbound-integration, etc.); >=80% of local scripts invoke their paired TypeScript verifier.

chunks.name: Governance and Timelock Operations
chunks.objective: Prevent governance capture, rushed changes, or role misconfiguration.

risks.title: Emergency override abuse
risks.problem: Emergency controls can be used outside intended threat conditions.
risks.evidence: DAO emergency actions require admin/emergencyApprover authorization, non-zero emergencyApprover, warmup delays, and secondary approval; EOA self-approval blocked via initiator check; explicit cancel paths for rescue and timelock replacement; EmergencyControl committee is member-gated, recovery requires halted system plus timelock and supermajority (memberCount-1). Verified in __tests__/governance/governance-timelock-operations-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Insufficient quorum floor enforcement
risks.problem: Parameter changes may reduce governance legitimacy or safety.
checks: Validate min quorum invariant under all paths
risks.evidence: Quorum floors enforced by code and tests: ABSOLUTE_MIN_QUORUM=500, emergency rescue requires new quorum >= current/10 and >= absolute floor, and strictly less than current; setParams bounds minVotes in [100, 1_000_000]; setMinParticipation bounds [3,100]; finalize requires both vote-point quorum and unique-voter participation. Verified in __tests__/governance/governance-timelock-operations-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Timelock admin handover risk
risks.problem: Admin transition can leave orphaned authority or weak ownership.
checks: Test handover sequence and rollback path
risks.evidence: SystemHandover.executeHandover requires armed state + maturity, reverts on second call, sets timelock admin to DAO, sets handoverExecuted=true, and burns dev authority via devMultisig=address(0); DAOTimelock setAdmin/setDelay are onlyTimelockSelf-gated with bounded delay rules and one-shot bounded emergencyReduceDelay. Verified in __tests__/governance/governance-timelock-operations-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Queue cancellation policy ambiguity
risks.problem: Lack of clear cancellation semantics can create governance disputes.
checks: Define cancellation authority and event semantics
risks.evidence: DAOTimelock cancellation semantics are explicit: cancel(bytes32) is onlyAdmin, rejects non-queued IDs, emits Cancelled(id), and removes queue tracking; execute is admin-only (front-run protection); cleanupExpired/requeueExpired are onlyAdmin; queue length capped at 500; DAO has explicit queued proposal expiry with ProposalStateChanged event. Verified in __tests__/governance/governance-timelock-operations-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Proposal payload validation gaps
risks.problem: Malformed or unexpected payloads could pass proposal creation.
checks: Validate proposal schema offchain and onchain
risks.evidence: DAO proposal creation validates target!=0, non-empty description, proposer cooldown, withdrawn-hash cooldown, and MAX_PROPOSALS cap; optional policy matrices enforce proposal-type target and selector allowlists with DAO_ProposalTargetNotAllowed/DAO_ProposalSelectorNotAllowed reverts; selector extraction safely handles short calldata (<4 bytes). Voting path enforces score-settlement/freshness constraints. Verified in __tests__/governance/governance-timelock-operations-audit.test.ts.

chunks.name: Payments, Commerce, and Escrow Flow
chunks.objective: Protect financial correctness through all payment lifecycle states.

risks.title: Escrow lock-time edge-case bypass
risks.problem: Unexpected trust-score transitions may bypass intended lock policy.
checks: Stress trust-score transitions during active escrow
risks.evidence: EscrowManager enforces trust-tier lock windows using cached scores (3d/7d/14d) with a hard MIN_LOCK_PERIOD=3 days floor; claimTimeout requires block.timestamp >= releaseTime; buyerClaimTimeout provides refund fallback after releaseTime + 30 days. Verified in __tests__/payments/commerce-escrow-audit.test.ts (R-056 tests + TS lock-floor model).

risks.title: Permit replay or overdraw
risks.problem: Merchant pull permits may be reused under race conditions.
risks.evidence: MerchantPortal scoped pull permits require maxAmount>0 and non-expired expiry; processPayment enforces approval flag, expiry window, remainingPull>=amount, and decrements merchantPullRemaining atomically; revoke path clears approval+remaining+expiry. Verified in __tests__/payments/commerce-escrow-audit.test.ts with monotonic allowance model and overdraw rejection checks.

risks.title: Partial payment settlement inconsistency
risks.problem: Split settlement states can orphan accounting entries.
risks.evidence: Escrow partial dispute path enforces DISPUTED state and buyerShareBps<=10000, computes buyerAmount=(amount*bps)/10000 and merchantAmount=amount-buyerAmount (conservation), and emits DisputeResolvedPartial; authority split enforces DAO-only for high-value and arbiter-only otherwise; timeoutResolve fallback handles deadlocked disputes after DISPUTE_TIMEOUT. Verified in __tests__/payments/commerce-escrow-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Auto-convert policy misconfiguration
risks.problem: Unsafe auto-convert settings can cause user losses under volatility.
risks.evidence: MerchantPortal constrains swap risk via MIN/MAX slippage bounds (9000-10000 bps), strict swap path checks (start token/end stablecoin/max path length), required stablecoin when router configured, and mandatory approval revocation after swap success/failure; quote_unavailable and swap_failed both trigger direct-settlement fallback with AutoConvertFallback events; merchant-side enable currently guarded with require(!enabled) to prevent unsafe rollout. Verified in __tests__/payments/commerce-escrow-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Payment replay in frontend retries
risks.problem: Duplicate submit behavior can trigger multiple chain transactions.
risks.evidence: Fixed: app/api/merchant/payments/confirm/route.ts now enforces DB-backed idempotency via merchant_payment_confirmations UNIQUE(merchant_address, tx_hash) with claim-before-dispatch semantics; duplicates return {success:true,idempotent:true,duplicate:true} and skip webhook dispatch. UI submit locks remain in app/pay/page.tsx and on-chain event verification remains enforced. Regression tests: __tests__/api/merchant/payments-confirm-idempotency.test.ts (2 passing tests).

chunks.name: Observability, Alerting, and Incident Response
chunks.objective: Detect and triage incidents before user impact escalates.

risks.title: Insufficient structured logging in critical flows
risks.problem: Missing context fields reduces incident diagnosability.
checks: Audit logs for tx hash, user, chain, route, error class
risks.evidence: Structured logging validated across logger and security APIs: lib/logger.ts normalizes context and forwards warning/error extras to Sentry; app/api/security/logs/route.ts enforces typed severity/type and persists structured fields (address,type,severity,message,details,user_agent,location,device_id,ip_hash); request correlation metadata (requestId/ipHash/ipSource) is attached. Verified in __tests__/observability/observability-incident-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Monitoring blind spots for edge/runtime failures
risks.problem: Some failure modes may not emit alerts.
checks: Map alert coverage to critical services
risks.evidence: Fixed: scripts/security-monitor-canary.ts adds synthetic probes for health/security paths and an explicit coverage map tied to R-062 critical services, producing security-monitor-canary-report.json and failing on probe/coverage gaps by default. .github/workflows/security-replay-monitor.yml now runs the canary hourly and uploads artifact security-monitor-canary-report. Coverage assertions validated in __tests__/observability/observability-incident-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: No SLO-backed alert thresholds
risks.problem: Alert fatigue or under-alerting due to arbitrary thresholds.
risks.evidence: Fixed: scripts/security-replay-metrics-report.ts now codifies SECURITY_MONITOR_REPLAY_REJECT_RATE_SLO, SECURITY_MONITOR_REPLAY_ERROR_BUDGET_24H, SECURITY_MONITOR_REPLAY_BURN_RATE_THRESHOLD, computes reject-rate/error-budget consumption/burnRate1h, and enforces fail-on-breach with SECURITY_MONITOR_FAIL_ON_SLO (default true). .github/workflows/security-replay-monitor.yml wires these env thresholds for hourly enforcement and artifacts. Policy checks validated in __tests__/observability/observability-incident-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Missing runbook links in alerts
risks.problem: On-call response slows without direct remediation guidance.
checks: Embed runbook URL in alert payloads
risks.evidence: Fixed: app/api/security/logs/route.ts now supports SECURITY_ALERT_RUNBOOK_URL and includes runbook_url in outbound critical alert webhook payloads, alongside requestId/ipHash/ipSource/timestamp context. Regression check updated in __tests__/observability/observability-incident-audit.test.ts and passing.

risks.title: Insufficient security replay analytics
risks.problem: Security incidents may lack historical replay for root-cause analysis.
risks.evidence: Replay analytics pipeline is complete: lib/security/webhookReplayTelemetry.ts records accepted/rejected events with reason/source/replay_key_hash into Postgres + retention cleanup; app/api/security/webhook-replay-metrics/route.ts exposes 1h/24h aggregates, top rejected sources, threshold triggering, and allowlisted access; scripts/security-replay-metrics-report.ts generates markdown reports and can fail on threshold; .github/workflows/security-replay-monitor.yml runs hourly and uploads artifacts. Verified in __tests__/observability/observability-incident-audit.test.ts.

chunks.name: Performance and Scalability
chunks.objective: Maintain predictable performance under realistic growth and attack load.

risks.title: Load-test coverage gaps for hot APIs
risks.problem: Critical endpoints may never be tested at realistic concurrency.
risks.evidence: Fixed: test/performance/endpoint-load-matrix.json now defines a machine-readable hot-endpoint load matrix with explicit sustained and spike profiles for critical API paths (health, governance proposals, merchant listings, seer analytics, replay metrics). test/performance/load.test.js imports this matrix and executes matrix-defined probes in the main scenario loop. Assertions are validated in __tests__/performance/performance-scalability-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Frontend long-task spikes on low-end devices
risks.problem: Complex screens can exceed interaction latency budgets.
checks: Capture Web Vitals and long tasks on mobile
risks.evidence: Fixed: lib/performance.ts now derives a DevicePerformanceProfile (hardwareConcurrency/deviceMemory/effective connection/saveData) and classifies low/standard/high tiers. observeLongTasks now supports threshold/profile options and emits tier-tagged long-task metrics. components/performance/PerformanceProvider.tsx now applies stricter long-task threshold for low-end sessions and marks low-end session telemetry. Verified in __tests__/performance/performance-scalability-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Cache invalidation inconsistencies
risks.problem: Stale data can produce incorrect balances or governance views.
checks: Map cache keys and invalidation triggers
risks.evidence: Fixed: centralized cache invalidation matrix added in lib/cache/cacheInvalidationPolicy.ts with canonical cache IDs, TTLs, query keys, and invalidation triggers across memory-map and React Query caches. hooks/usePriceHooks.ts, hooks/useENS.ts, and components/wallet/Web3Provider.tsx now consume this policy directly for stale/cache lifetimes, reducing policy drift across hot UI data paths. Validated in __tests__/performance/performance-scalability-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Rate limiter saturation behavior unknown
risks.problem: Hard throttling may trigger cascading user-visible failures.
risks.evidence: Fixed: endpoint-load-matrix.json includes a dedicated rateLimiterSaturation constant-arrival-rate scenario; test/performance/load.test.js wires this scenario into k6 options and tracks gracefulDegradationHits for throttling statuses (429/503) with threshold coverage. Validation assertions exist in __tests__/performance/performance-scalability-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: No budget enforcement in CI for perf regressions
risks.problem: Performance regresses gradually without hard guardrails.
checks: Enforce Lighthouse and route budget thresholds
risks.evidence: Fixed: .github/workflows/release-gate.yml now runs a dedicated Performance budget gate step (`npm run -s test:performance`) in the quality gate. test:performance invokes build + @lhci/cli autorun, and lighthouserc.json enforces lighthouse-budget.json thresholds, creating CI hard-fail budget enforcement for regressions. Verified by __tests__/performance/performance-scalability-audit.test.ts.

chunks.name: Supply Chain and Dependency Risk
chunks.objective: Prevent dependency-originated vulnerabilities and behavior drift.

risks.title: Transitive dependency vulnerability exposure
risks.problem: Large dependency graph increases latent CVE surface.
risks.evidence: npm audit --omit=dev reports 6 production vulnerabilities (2 moderate, 4 high), confirming active transitive exposure to triage.

risks.title: Semver range drift causes runtime changes
risks.problem: Caret ranges can introduce behavior shifts without explicit review.
risks.evidence: Fixed: critical runtime packages are now pinned to exact versions in package.json (next/react/react-dom/wagmi/viem/@tanstack/react-query/@rainbow-me/rainbowkit/@sentry/nextjs/web-vitals/pg/jsonwebtoken). scripts/verify-supply-chain-controls.ts enforces exact pinning for this critical set to prevent silent runtime drift from caret/tilde ranges. Verified by __tests__/supply-chain/supply-chain-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Toolchain version mismatch across environments
risks.problem: Node/npm mismatches produce non-reproducible builds.
risks.evidence: package.json now defines engines (node >=20 <23, npm >=10) to enforce runtime/toolchain compatibility.

risks.title: Unreviewed postinstall script behaviors
risks.problem: Scripts can perform unsafe actions in developer or CI environments.
risks.evidence: Fixed: lifecycle behavior is now policy-checked by scripts/verify-supply-chain-controls.ts, which audits lifecycle scripts (preinstall/install/postinstall/prepare/prebuild/etc.) against restricted patterns (curl/wget/bash -c/node -e/npm install mutations). package.json postinstall remains limited to validate:env flow. Verified by __tests__/supply-chain/supply-chain-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Artifact patch drift under manual edits
risks.problem: Manual patch files may go stale with upstream package updates.
risks.evidence: Fixed: scripts/verify-supply-chain-controls.ts now performs patch freshness checks by running git apply --check against every file under patches/. Security CI now runs npm run -s security:supply-chain in .github/workflows/security.yml to enforce patch apply validity on every push/PR. Verified by __tests__/supply-chain/supply-chain-audit.test.ts.

chunks.name: Governance Process and Human Operations
chunks.objective: Reduce human error in high-impact operational actions.

risks.title: Missing two-person review for critical operations
risks.problem: Single-operator actions increase irreversible mistake risk.
risks.evidence: Dual-approval is now policy-as-code via docs/operations/CRITICAL_OPERATION_DUAL_APPROVAL_POLICY.md plus machine-checkable artifacts audit/critical-operation-policy.json and audit/critical-operation-signoff.latest.json. scripts/verify-dual-approval-policy.ts (npm run -s ops:dual-approval) enforces minimum distinct approvers, required role coverage, decision/evidence completeness, and policy-version matching. CI gate added in .github/workflows/release-gate.yml and verified by __tests__/operations/accepted-risk-closure-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Runbook drift from actual scripts
risks.problem: Docs may not match current command behavior.
checks: Replay runbook steps in clean environment
Update docs and add drift checks
risks.evidence: Automated runbook drift verification is now enforced by scripts/verify-runbook-drift.ts (npm run -s ops:runbook:drift), checking required commands in LOCAL_VALIDATION_RUNBOOK.md and docs/SEER_AUTONOMOUS_ACTIVATION_RUNBOOK.md plus npm script existence. CI enforcement added in .github/workflows/release-gate.yml and covered by __tests__/operations/governance-operations-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Incomplete rollback procedures
risks.problem: Failed rollouts may not have tested rollback paths.
risks.evidence: A universal rollback runbook is now documented at docs/operations/ROLLBACK_DRILL_RUNBOOK.md, with executable readiness checks in scripts/verify-rollback-readiness.ts (npm run -s ops:rollback:readiness) and structured drill evidence in audit/rollback-drill.latest.json. The gate is enforced in .github/workflows/release-gate.yml and validated by __tests__/operations/governance-operations-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Unclear release stop/go criteria
risks.problem: Ambiguous readiness thresholds can permit risky launches.
risks.evidence: Measurable release criteria and signoff artifacts are now codified in audit/release-stop-go.criteria.json and audit/release-stop-go.signoff.json, enforced by scripts/verify-release-stop-go.ts (npm run -s ops:release:stop-go). Release workflow now executes this stop/go gate in .github/workflows/release-gate.yml, with verification coverage in __tests__/operations/governance-operations-audit.test.ts.

risks.title: Incident communication protocol gaps
risks.problem: Delayed stakeholder communication worsens incident impact.
risks.evidence: Incident communication protocol is now explicitly defined at docs/operations/INCIDENT_COMMUNICATION_PROTOCOL.md, including severity matrix, escalation tree, required message templates, and tabletop cadence. A structured tabletop artifact was added at audit/incident-communication-tabletop.latest.json and validated via __tests__/operations/governance-operations-audit.test.ts.
